title: Market Brief — Thursday, May 21, 2026
summary: GPU compute is severely tight across every class. Util-avail stands at just 14% for A100, 12% for H100, 10% for H200, and a critical 3% for B200, meaning nearly all rented capacity is spoken for. B200 spot jumped 2.6% day-over-day to $5.13/hr while H100 cratered 11.6% to $2.33/hr, signaling a demand rotation into next-gen silicon as legacy supply loosens marginally.
paragraphs: GPU compute is severely tight across every class. Util-avail stands at just 14% for A100, 12% for H100, 10% for H200, and a critical 3% for B200, meaning nearly all rented capacity is spoken for. B200 spot jumped 2.6% day-over-day to $5.13/hr while H100 cratered 11.6% to $2.33/hr, signaling a demand rotation into next-gen silicon as legacy supply loosens marginally.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), serving Llama 70B on H100 runs roughly $8.54/M tokens, yet medium-tier providers sell at $3/M, burning about $5.54 per million tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output is deep underwater on any GPU besides maybe B200 at scale. Grades: OpenAI Strong (premium tiers plus gpt-oss at $0.18/M on optimized infra), Anthropic Neutral (Opus margins healthy, Haiku tight), Google Neutral (Flash well-priced), DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 at 96.7% utilization and 3% availability with 109% annualized price volatility is overvalued for spot buyers chasing short-term capacity. A100 at $1.07/hr spot with a 12.2pp utilization surge is the undervalued play: lock in reserved A100 clusters now before the tightening trend closes the remaining 14% availability window.
bullets: 3% util-avail on B200 marks the tightest GPU supply across all classes tracked by Ornn.
12% util-avail on H100 qualifies as severe shortage despite an 11.6% spot price drop today.
96.7% B200 utilization is up 7.1 percentage points in 30 days, the fastest ramp in the stack.
$0.18/M output for OpenAI gpt-oss-120b is 97% below the LLMflation index baseline of $60/M.
160.2% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest GPU to hold on spot contracts.
$41.17 per DDR5 16Gb chip held nearly flat at 0.16% week-over-week, signaling stable memory costs.
